item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hi, thanks for the review! I can attempt to address some of your concerns with the piece here.

Open
Close

The conprocs don't make sense, there's a ban on electronics and metals, but there's no clear reason why this is the case. The masks, where are they stored? Separately? Why is a prismatic shaft required, what would be wrong with a viewing window?

So, the reasoning behind the ban on electronics is that SCP-4064 communicates via electronic impulses, and since their 'abilities' aren't fully understood, the Foundation wants to limit their ability to spread communication as effectively as possible.

Makes me hurt… you're throwing in sciencey sounding words to add weight but you're using them wrong.

The aforementioned explanation is also the reasoning for the ban on ferromagnetic metals, which are actually a fairly common term used to describe metals like iron, nickel and cobalt, which are able to produce a magnetic charge. Magnetic charges are inherently related to electronic charges, hence the association.

Breaks suspension that SCP would do this, either the dr. wouldn't be punished in the first place (suspended pending review, for example) or the reprimand would stick (SCP doesn't care if it was cause an anomaly got you, it just means you f*ked up to let it happen).

The Foundation can be interpreted in a lot of ways, but in almost all of them, it is represented as cold and unfeeling. Cold and unfeeling, however, doesn't mean cruel. Dr. Rouslin clearly broke protocol, and was reprimanded as such, but after more info on the SCP was discovered, they removed it from his record. This is, at least in my eyes, fairly normal sounding, and non-contradictory.

That's everything that wasn't strictly opinion based, and I hope it offered some helpful insight as to the choices I made when writing this. Thanks again for the review!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wound up skimming. I came backwards through parts to see if I missed anything, and nope.

+ show block
– hide block

One problem for me is that it is a character piece about a collective anomaly, and that's not really realized.

The conprocs don't make sense, there's a ban on electronics and metals, but there's no clear reason why this is the case. The masks, where are they stored? Separately? Why is a prismatic shaft required, what would be wrong with a viewing window?

ferromagnetic charge

Makes me hurt… you're throwing in sciencey sounding words to add weight but you're using them wrong.

It's just that the containment is leading me to believe that there's some real need for all of it… and there isn't. That's my major dislike here.

Some nitpicks:
Footnote 2 is a bit redundant.

but since the discovery of SCP-4064's emotional properties, it has been lifted from his record.

Breaks suspension that SCP would do this, either the dr. wouldn't be punished in the first place (suspended pending review, for example) or the reprimand would stick (SCP doesn't care if it was cause an anomaly got you, it just means you f*ked up to let it happen).

The interviews and addenda just don't hit me hard at all. The conprocs had me looking right and the narrative was turning left.


-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Unique take on it, I really like this! At the start I worried it had overtones of "scary ladies who sex you to death" but it quickly departed from there and became something different entirely.

It took me a while to understand why Dr. Rouslin called in — was it because he had been duped by 4064? As in, the sisters had since left him heartbroken and he was just coming to his senses? A little unclear. But his confession, like MrMeenor said, is really well-delivered and feels very natural for a recovering Foundation employee.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow, definitely liked this one through the whole article. Kept me engaged on it.

On that note now I am curious on the other daughters.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I appreciate it! As for the last line…

show
hide

It's supposed to imply that she's aging. She's going to be trapped in that room, bitter and alone for the rest of her natural life, mindlessly believing that her Sisters will return.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Unique characterization work, I like it. I'm not really sure what the last line was trying to imply but that confusion didn't spoil the rest of the work for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The toss up of,

SCP-4064-B should not under any circumstances be made aware that this change has been put into effect.

Followed by the spike,

SCP-4064 is a hive-mind, capable of inhabiting six distinct bodies.

Killed me in all the right ways. This was a great story. For a second I was worried it'd just be about some sort of super powerful spooky ladies, then about an only near powerless SCP whose only anomalous effects was its face mask. I loved where it ended up though, I also appreciate the Dr.'s account of their effect on him, it felt very real and in distress. Easily +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow.

Anatolia is like the Devouring Anima as a Foundationverse deity.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
This is… This is Doctor Henry Rouslin, Site-96 General Research and Containment, Level 3 security clearance, Identification Code: H-373701. The purpose of this Author Post is to give credit where credit is due, so let’s just jump into it.

Image Credit:

+ Link
- Link

https://www.nicepik.com/woman-with-gold-colored-mask-while-topless-model-paint-hair-fashion-shoot-studio-portrait-free-photo-817714


Also thanks to Ayers for condensing the size of the image.

Critter Credit:
Thanks to Blacknife09, zesper, Popsioak, ODSTDRAGON, KnightAlaska, N_Aepic_Fael, and KindlyTurtleClem for giving critique and helping grow this piece into what it is now.
